title: Thermal Conductivity Measurement of Aluminum Alloy
abstract: This experiment measures the thermal conductivity of an aluminum alloy sample under controlled conditions.
introduction: Thermal conductivity is an important property for engineering materials, particularly in heat transfer applications.
objective: To determine the thermal conductivity coefficient of aluminum alloy 6061-T6.
methodology: The experiment uses the steady-state heat flow method with temperature sensors placed at regular intervals along the sample.
observations: Sample showed uniform heating across the length
analysis: The data shows consistent thermal properties with literature values.
conclusion: The measured thermal conductivity of 167 W/m·K is within 2% of the reference value for 6061-T6 aluminum alloy.
references: 1. Incropera, F.P. (2020). Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer. Wiley.
interpretation: Results confirm the material meets specifications for thermal applications.
next_steps: Test other aluminum alloys for comparison
qa_preparation: Be prepared to discuss uncertainty analysis and comparison with other measurement techniques.
